comment: Single source of truth for the test matrix. Backends x suites. The runner (run.mjs), the aggregator (aggregate.mjs) and CI all read this.

backends.label: JavaScriptCore
backends.comment: WebKit JSCOnly built from source. The shippable engine.

suites.label: rusty_v8 API
suites.comment: Vendored rusty_v8 integration tests run unmodified against our v8 shim (see [[test]] entries in Cargo.toml). Cheapest: no deno.
suites.ignore_comment: concurrent_isolate_creation_and_disposal races and SIGABRTs ~75% of CI runs on quickjs (Linux) — a genuine concurrency flake that was blocking every PR. Quarantined on quickjs until the underlying isolate create/dispose race is fixed.

suites.label: deno_core
suites.comment: cargo test -p deno_core in the patched deno checkout. Links our v8 shim but does NOT build the full deno binary.
suites.ignore_comment: Known-flaky / platform-broken tests, quarantined from the ratchet: NEVER run (skipped up-front) and excluded from pass/fail/baseline, so the cell is deterministic. `ignore` = all backends; `ignore_by_backend` = per-backend. Bare libtest paths (no `deno_core::` prefix). On quickjs (Linux CI) a cluster of `runtime::ops::tests::*` op-ABI tests crash non-deterministically (SIGSEGV/SIGBUS), terminate/heap loops hang (no-op TerminateExecution + no heap watchdog), and dyn_import_recurse_err ASLR-stack-overflows; together their random abort points made the whole single-process run order-dependent. Quarantining the observed-unstable set makes quickjs/deno_core reproducible. The JSC backends do NOT need these (dyn_import fixed by #22; terminate/heap handled by the watchdog on vendor_jsc).
